title: THE RECORDINGS — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2 / OPERATION REVISION

designRules.objective: Guide Hale from the relay room to the archive sector, recover Incident Alpha evidence, and decide whether to keep 7.14 sealed.
designRules.choiceRule: Every visible choice group has exactly two options.
designRules.puzzlePolicy: Matching puzzle removed. Episode uses one decrypt puzzle plus operational decisions.
designRules.systemMessagePolicy: System messages are minimized and used only for terminal-critical events.
designRules.trustPolicy: Trust changes are never announced as UI text; they are felt through dialogue, hesitation, cooperation, and future flags.

decrypt_disk_02.title: ARCHIVE DECRYPTION
decrypt_disk_02.description: Encrypted archive storage detected. Hale must stay near the terminal while you recover the access key.
hints: THE ARCHIVE KEY IS PRINTED AS A HINT

events.text: You saved the Door 12 records. I wish that made me feel better.
It doesn't.
I'm leaving the relay room now. Archive sector is two decks down, assuming the lifts are still pretending to be dead.
Objective is simple: reach the archive, find the missing Incident Alpha package, and leave before the station notices we are interested.
I said simple. I did not say sane.

choices.text: WHAT ARE YOU NOT TELLING ME?

events.text: That is fair.
I hate that it is fair.
I remember deleting something called Incident Alpha. I remember my hand shaking. I do not remember why I pressed confirm.
So if I start sounding too certain, do me a favor and don't believe me immediately.
Good. Movement first, existential crisis second.
I am stepping into the service corridor. Lights are dead on the left side. The right side is doing that charming flicker that makes every shadow look employed.
If I start narrating too much, that's not for drama. It's because silence has been a terrible roommate.

events.title: ARCHIVE APPROACH
events.subtitle: Hale is moving from relay spine access toward archive sector junction

events.text: Junction reached. There are two ways down.
Main corridor is faster, open, and full of broken ceiling panels.
Service ladder is slower, tighter, and smells like hot plastic. Weirdly, that makes it the more honest option.
Main corridor it is.
I'm moving quickly. Not running. Running is how people in training videos become examples.

event.text: I'm fine. Panel fell behind me. I am aggressively fine.
event.speaker: HALE

events.text: Archive door is ahead. Suit pressure dipped for a second, but it recovered.
Service ladder. Sensible. Miserable, but sensible.

events.title: SERVICE LADDER DESCENT
events.subtitle: Hale is descending through a narrow maintenance shaft

events.text: Found a spare filter clipped inside the shaft. Old, but sealed.
I am choosing to interpret that as the station apologizing. Bad apology, useful gift.
Archive access door is in front of me.
The panel is awake. That is rarely good news. Sleeping panels are lazy. Awake panels have opinions.
I can scan it first, or I can force the access panel before the security routine finishes waking up.
Scanning.
Good call. There is a quarantine latch on the inner frame. If I had forced it, the archive would have locked itself and probably judged us while doing it.
Bypassing latch now. Slowly. Carefully. Two words I have started appreciating very late in life.
Forcing it.
Door didn't like that.
I jammed the quarantine latch, but I got inside. That was technically success. It just arrived wearing bruises.
Next time I say a panel is awake, assume it is vindictive.
I'm inside archive sector C.
Rows of dead storage columns. Frost on the floor. Someone left this place in a hurry.
There. Portable storage device. Still powered.

events.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
events.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decrypt attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked
events.caption: A portable archive device recovered inside Sector C.

events.text: It wants a key. Of course it wants a key.
Wrong key. Archive noticed.
Don't guess. The device is not clever. It is bureaucratic. Bureaucracy loves exact wording.
I can keep the quarantine loop busy for a moment, but not forever.

choices.text: ASK HALE TO READ THE DEVICE AGAIN.

events.text: Reading it again.
Archive Key Hint: ALPHA.
Not Incident Alpha. Not a clever abbreviation. Just ALPHA.
Sometimes terror is hidden behind a label. Which is rude, but efficient.

choices.text: LET HALE STEADY THE TERMINAL FIRST.

events.text: Thank you.
My hands were starting to shake. I was pretending they weren't, but apparently the terminal has better eyesight than my pride.
Ready. Try it now.

events.title: INCIDENT ALPHA TRANSCRIPT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:15 — Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:19 — Signal interruption.
21:20 — Casualty report created.
21:22 — Archive deletion command issued.
events.caption: Recovered transcript from the encrypted archive device.
events.message: [INCIDENT ALPHA TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That recording should not exist.
And before you ask— yes, I know how terrible that sentence sounds.
21:22. Archive deletion command issued.
I remember a deletion prompt. I do not remember why I accepted it.

choices.text: WHY DOES THE FILE STILL EXIST?

events.text: Because either I failed to delete it...
or someone wanted me to believe I had.
I remember sitting at a terminal. I remember my hand shaking before I confirmed the deletion.
I don't remember the reason. That missing reason is starting to feel bigger than the file.
Right. Moving.
There are camera analysis terminals on the next row. If the transcript is true, there should be a visual trace.
I would like the visual trace to not be me.
I am aware that hope is not a method.

events.title: ARCHIVE ROW CROSSING
events.subtitle: Hale is moving between storage columns toward the camera analysis terminal

events.text: Terminal reached. It has the Door 12 feed, but the actual frame is shredded.
I can inspect the raw corruption myself, or you can let the station run automated analysis.
I hate this plan, which probably means it's useful.
There is a suited figure outside Door 12.
Engineering outline. Right hand near the manual override.
I want to say it isn't me.
I can't.
Automated analysis. Cold, fast, impersonal.
Honestly, that might be kinder.
Running it now.

events.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
events.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame unrecoverable.
Automated analysis result:
- One suited figure detected.
- Suit outline compatible with ECHO engineering issue.
- Right-hand object: manual override handle.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity confidence: 64%
events.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted camera data. No image recovered.

events.text: Sixty-four percent.
Enough to ruin a life. Not enough to prove one.
The system is pointing at me with a broken finger.
There should be an override ledger beside the camera terminal.
I'm checking it now.

events.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
events.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
events.caption: Recovered security ledger for Door 12 and connected commands.
events.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Valid authorization. Unknown source.
That is not a category. That's a threat wearing a badge.
Maybe.
I hate that the honest answer is maybe.
But if it was me, why redact myself? Why hide the source and leave the outline?
Either I was protecting someone, or someone was building a trail back to me.
Searching for non-human source patterns.
There. Something rode the emergency channel wake command.
It did not open the door. It waited until the door was open.
That is worse. I am running out of ways for things to become worse, and yet they remain creative.
We have three things now.
A deletion command. A Hale-shaped figure. A valid authorization from nowhere.
I can trace the deletion chain, or we can open the emergency channel note connected to the wake command.
For the record, I dislike both options equally. Very democratic dread.
Tracing deletion chain.
Command issued after casualty report. Before emergency channel wake completed.
So whoever deleted Incident Alpha knew someone was dead, and knew the channel was waking up.
That makes it less like cleanup and more like containment.
Opening emergency note.
I already regret saying that out loud.

events.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
events.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.
events.caption: Recovered emergency channel instruction connected to Incident Alpha.
events.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: No.
Nobody was supposed to find that channel.
Backup channels are supposed to wait for you to call them.
This one answered before anyone transmitted.
That is not a backup. That is a mouth.
Good.
Thank you for not making me beg. I was prepared to, but I would have hated both of us for it.
Operator... the note says not to open it if a return signal already exists.
We already have one.
You are talking to it.

choices.text: LET HALE REST FOR A MINUTE.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR
No. Close it. Close it now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Operator, listen to my voice. Not the channel. Mine.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: TOO EARLY
Sealed. Mostly.
There is no such thing as mostly sealed in a healthy system, so let's enjoy what that says about us.
We keep moving. Personnel records next. I need to know who else the station thinks is dead.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: I KNOW YOUR SIGNAL NOW
What did you just do?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Don't answer. I know what you did.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Personnel records. Now. Before it learns anything else from us.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Personnel records are on the next deck.
If Incident Alpha is the wound, personnel is where the station wrote down the body count.
I am not ready for that.
So obviously we are going.
A minute?
I forgot people can offer those.
Okay. I'm sitting against the archive wall. Just for sixty seconds.
I can hear the station settling around me. For a second it almost sounds like rain.
I miss rain.
There. Minute over. Personnel records.

choices.text: ASK ABOUT RAIN WHILE HE MOVES.

changes: Converted the episode from chat/archive reading into a real-time rescue operation.
Removed the matching puzzle and replaced it with operational evidence decisions.
Kept only one decrypt puzzle with safe retry and hint flows.
Every visible choice group has exactly two choices.
Hale physically moves from relay room to archive sector, crosses junctions, accesses terminals, and reacts to the environment.
System messages are minimized; trust changes are represented through dialogue, flags, stress, and later consequences.
Choices create meaningful branches: route choice, door approach, evidence handling, 7.14 decision, rest vs push forward.
Bad 7.14 path continues with long-term consequences instead of hard-stopping.
Character emotion, fatigue, memory gaps, hesitation, and sarcasm are integrated into action.
coreRulesApplied: Somut hedef: Archive sector'a ulaş, Incident Alpha kayıtlarını kurtar, 7.14 kararını ver.
Karakterler doğal, duygusal ve tutarlı konuşuyor.
Her choice group tam olarak iki seçenek.
Bazı sonuçlar geri döndürülemez: opened714Early, unknownHeardOperator.
